According to an opinion expressed
yesterday by T. S. Heskett. local or
gani2er for the American Federation
of Labor. one of the most important
measures adopted at the recent con-
vention of the Washington State Fed
Grafton of Labor has not yet been
drawn to the attention of the public.
Three fraternal delegates were APP
pointed to attend the Oregon State
Labor convention to be held at La
Grande on May 10. the purpose being
to have a committee selected from
Washington, Oregon and California to
act as n kind of Information bureau
between these three states. The Cali
fornia State Labor convention was
tee. and the end in view appears to
be to put an end to unfair labor com
petition in neighboring states by
bringing about a uniform scale of
wages among unions of the same
craft. For instance, II the carpen-
ters should succeed in raising the
wage scale or be conceded reduc-
tion in working hours, this will i'm
mediately be communicated by the
bureau to unions of the same craft in
each of the three states. The dele
gates chosen at the convention of the
Washington Federation of Labor were
Il. 8. Heskett OF Spokane. II. w.
North of Tacoma and Il. A. Rust of
Seattle.
